title: Articulating seating system with integrated lifting device
abstract: An integrated lifting device that is assembled with a seating system in a vehicle. The integrated lifting device includes a personal mobile device receiving portion configured to receive a personal mobile device and a user having limited mobility. The seating system can include a user seat for the user to transfer from a wheelchair, etc., to the user seat in the vehicle. A second seating system can be positioned adjacent the first seating system to receive the user. The personal mobile device receiving portion receives the wheelchair and transports the wheelchair into the vehicle. Alternatively to the integrated lifting device, a hoist platform and hoist assembly can lift the wheelchair and user into the vehicle. The vehicle can include logic for the coordination of the movements of the wheelchair lifting device and the seating system.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. An integrated lifting device for assembly with a seating system assembled with a vehicle, the integrated lifting device comprising: a personal mobile device receiving portion configured to receive a personal mobile device, the personal mobile device receiving portion configured for assembly or attachment to the seating system. 2. The integrated lifting device of claim 1, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion has a rectangular shape. 3. The integrated lifting device of claim 1, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion has one of these shapes: square, semi-circular, trapezoidal, or some combination of these geometric shapes. 4. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion is made of a material that is strong enough to support the personal mobile device. 5. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion has a thickness sufficient to support the personal mobile device. 6. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the seating system is an articulated seating system. 7. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the seating system includes a user seat. 8. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the seating system is movable between an extension position and a drive position. 9. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion is movable between an extension position and a drive position. 10. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion is mounted or attached to any one of a front, a side, or a back of the seating system. 11. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion is positioned in one of a forward position of the seating system, a side position of the seating system, or rearward position of the seating system. 12. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion is sized to receive the personal mobile device that is any of a wheelchair, two or more wheelchairs, a manual wheelchair, a powerchair, a scooter, or a manual folding wheelchair. 13. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion includes an extension end opposite an attachment end with a body portion that spans between the extension and attachment ends. 14. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion includes an extension end having a width that is sufficient to receive the personal mobile device. 15. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion includes a body portion having a length and a width that are sufficient to receive the personal mobile device. 16. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion includes a body portion having a top surface configured to receive one or more devices to receive, secure, and restrain the personal mobile device. 17. The integrated lifting device of claim 16, wherein the one or more devices include any of a securement strap, a docking system for a wheelchair, a bracket, a hold down bar, one or more wheel chocks, a cargo barrier, or one or more fasteners or bolts. 18. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion is integrated onto the seating system to support and transport the personal mobility device from an interior space of a vehicle to exterior of the vehicle, and vice versa. 19. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion is integrated onto the seating system to support and transport the personal mobility device from an interior space of a vehicle through a door opening of the vehicle to exterior of the vehicle, or vice versa. 20. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion is configured to move between an expanded configuration and a compact configuration. 21. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion is configured to fold one or more portions of itself for storage. 22. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion includes one or more sections configured to bend to decrease the size of the personal mobile device. 23. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the seating system is mounted in a mid-right position of the vehicle relative to a door opening of the vehicle, and the personal 

mobile device receiving portion is assembled with the seating system in a front mount position or a side mount position relative to the door opening of the vehicle. 24. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the seating system is mounted in a front mounting position relative to a door opening of the vehicle; and wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion is adequately sized and positioned in front of the seating system such that the seating system and the personal mobile device receiving portion are configured to move together through the door opening of the vehicle. 25. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the vehicle is an autonomous vehicle. 26. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the seating system is mounted in a side mounting position relative to a door opening of the vehicle; and wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion is positioned adjacent the seating system. 27. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion includes an attachment end that is configured for assembly with the seating system. 28. The integrated lifting device of claim 27, wherein the attachment end includes one or more openings sized to receive one or more fasteners for assembly with the seating system. 29. The integrated lifting device of claim 27, wherein the attachment end is welded to the seating system. 30. The integrated lifting device of claim 27, wherein the attachment end includes one or more sliding mechanisms for assembly with one or more members of the seating system to lock the attachment end to the seating system. 31. The integrated lifting device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion travels with the seating system, such that when the seating system is deployed outside of the vehicle, the personal mobile device receiving portion is level on the ground in an access position allowing the personal mobile device to be maneuvered onto the personal mobile device receiving portion. 32. An integrated lifting device for assembly with a vehicle, the integrated lifting device comprising: a personal mobile device receiving portion configured to receive a personal mobile device, the personal mobile device receiving portion configured for assembly or attachment to a first seating system 

assembled with the vehicle, wherein a second seating system is positioned adjacent the first seating system. 33. The integrated lifting device of claim 32, wherein the first and second seating systems are each an articulated seating system. 34. The integrated lifting device of any of claims 32 -33, wherein the vehicle is an autonomous vehicle. 35. The integrated lifting device of claims 32- 34, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion and the first seating system are positioned in a first row of the vehicle and the second seating system including a user seat is positioned in a second row of the vehicle, wherein a vehicle opening of the vehicle is configured to receive the first and second seating systems, the personal mobile device receiving portion, and the user seat. 36. The integrated lifting device of any of claims 32-34, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion and the first seating system are positioned in a second row of the vehicle and the second seating system including a user seat is positioned in a third row of the vehicle, wherein a vehicle opening of the vehicle is configured to receive the first and second seating systems, the personal mobile device receiving portion, and the user seat. 37. The integrated lifting device of any of claims 32-34, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion and the first seating system are positioned in a second row of the vehicle and the second seating system including a user seat is positioned in a third row of the vehicle, wherein a vehicle opening of the vehicle is configured to receive the first and second seating systems, the personal mobile device receiving portion, and the user seat. 38. The integrated lifting device of any of claims 32-34, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion and the first seating system are positioned in one row of the vehicle and the second seating system including a user seat is positioned in the one row of the vehicle adjacent the first seating system. 39. The integrated lifting device of any of claims 32-38, wherein the vehicle can be a van, an autonomous vehicle, or another type of vehicle. 40. The integrated lifting device of any of claims 32-39, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion is assembled with the second seating system instead of the first seating system. 41. In one method of operation of any of claims 32-40, wherein the first and the second seating systems with the personal mobile device receiving portion deploy together. 42. In one method of operation of any of claims 32-40, wherein the first and the second seating systems with the personal mobile device receiving portion deploy independently of each other. 43. In one method of operation of any of claims 32-42, wherein the first and the second seating systems with the personal mobile device receiving portion are moved from a secured drive position to an extension position wherein the first and the second seating systems with the personal mobile device receiving portion extend through a vehicle opening of the vehicle. 44. In one method of operation of any of claims 32-43, wherein the first and the second seating systems with the personal mobile device receiving portion move from a drive position to an access position that is outside the vehicle. 45. In one method of operation of any of claims 32-44, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion supports and transports a personal mobile device with or without an occupant from outside of the vehicle to inside the vehicle. 46. In one method of operation of any of claims 32-45, wherein the personal mobile device receiving portion is configured to stow or store the personable mobile device. 47. In one method of operation of any of claims 32-46, wherein the first seating system being deployed outside of the vehicle, the personal mobile device receiving portion is substantially level on the ground in an access position to receive a personal mobile device thereon. 48. In one method of operation of any of claims 32-47, wherein the first and second seating systems travel from an access position that is outside of the vehicle to a drive position that is inside of the vehicle. 49. An apparatus for moving a personal mobile device into a vehicle, the apparatus comprising: a hoist positioned adjacent a seating system mounted in the vehicle, the hoist configured to receive a personal mobile device; and a lifting mechanism assembled with the hoist, wherein the lifting mechanism is configured to move the hoist and the personal mobile device between an interior of the vehicle and an exterior of the vehicle. 50. The apparatus of claim 49, wherein the hoist is configured to stow the personal mobile device. 51. The apparatus of any of claims 49-50, wherein the hoist has any one of these shapes including a rectangle, a square, a semi-circular, a trapezoid, or some combination of these geometric shapes. 52. The apparatus of any of claims 49-51, wherein the hoist is made of a material that can support the personal mobile device. 53. The apparatus of any of claims 49-52, wherein the hoist includes a body portion that spans between an extension end and an attachment end. 54. The apparatus of claim 53, wherein the body portion includes a top surface that is configured with one or more devices to secure the personal mobile device in place while the vehicle is in motion. 55. The apparatus of claim 54, wherein the one or more devices include any of a securement strap, a docking system, a bracket, a hold down bar, one or more wheel chocks, a cargo barrier, one or more fasteners, and/or one or more bolts. 56. The apparatus of any of claims 53-55, wherein the attachment end is configured for assembly with the lifting mechanism. 57. The apparatus of claim 56, wherein the attachment end includes one or more openings sized to receive one or more fasteners for assembly with the lifting mechanism. 58. The apparatus of claim 56, wherein the attachment end is welded to the lifting mechanism 59. The apparatus of claim 56, wherein the attachment end is assembled with the lifting mechanism by one or more sliding mechanisms that allow interlocking between the attachment end and one or more members of the lifting mechanism. 60. The apparatus of any of claims 49-59, wherein the lifting mechanism assembled with the hoist is positioned in a first row of the vehicle and the seating system including a user seat is positioned in a second row of the vehicle, wherein a vehicle opening of the vehicle is configured to receive the hoist, the seating system, and the user seat. 61. The apparatus of any of claims 49-59, wherein the lifting mechanism assembled with the hoist is positioned in a second row of the vehicle and the seating system including a user seat is positioned in a third row of the vehicle, wherein a vehicle opening of the vehicle is configured to receive the hoist, the seating system, and the user seat. 62. The apparatus of any of claims 49-59, wherein the vehicle is any of a van, an autonomous vehicle, or another type of vehicle. 63. In one method of operation of any of claims 49-62, wherein the seating system and the hoist deploy together. 64. In one method of operation of any of claims 49-62, wherein the seating system and the hoist deploy independently of each other. 65. In one method of operation of any of claims 49-64, wherein the seating system and the hoist move from a drive position to an access position that is outside the vehicle. 66. In one method of operation of any of claims 49-64, wherein the hoist is configured to stow or store the personal mobile device. 67. In one method of operation of any of claims 32-48, a programmable sequence for maneuvering the integrated wheelchair lifting device with first seating system and a second seating system. 68. In one method of operation of claim 67, wherein the vehicle has an entry too narrow for the integrated wheelchair lifting device with first seating system and a second seating system to pass through simultaneously. 69. In one method of operation of any claims 67-68, wherein the integrated wheelchair lifting device with first seating system transfers from the secured drive position to deployed outside of the vehicle, and subsequently the second seating system transfers from the secured drive position to deployed outside of the vehicle, or vice versa. 70. In one method of operation of any claims 67-68, wherein the integrated wheelchair lifting device with first seating system transfers from deployed outside of the vehicle to the secured drive position, and subsequently the second seating system transfers from deployed outside of the vehicle to the secured drive position, or vice versa. 71. In one method of operation of any claims 67-70, wherein the secondly operated system transfer starts before the firstly operated system transfer is concluded. 72. In one method of operation of any of claims 49-66, a programmable sequence for maneuvering the seating system and the wheelchair hoist. 73. In one method of operation of claim 71, wherein the vehicle has an entry too narrow for the seating system and the wheelchair hoist to pass through simultaneously. 74. In one method of operation of any claims 71-72, wherein the seating system transfers from the secured drive position to deployed outside of the vehicle, and subsequently wheelchair hoist transfers from the secured drive position to deployed outside of the vehicle, or vice versa. 75. In one method of operation of any claims 67-68, wherein the integrated seating system transfers from deployed outside of the vehicle to the secured drive position, and subsequently the 

wheelchair hoist transfers from deployed outside of the vehicle to the secured drive position, or vice versa. 76. In one method of operation of any claims 71-74, wherein the secondly operated system transfer starts before the firstly operated system transfer is concluded. 77. An accessible vehicle having a door opening with a door comprising: a lifting device having a PMD receiving portion configured to receive a PMD, the PMD receiving portion being movable from a stow position at an interior of the vehicle through the door opening to a deployed position at an exterior of the vehicle; a seating system with a passenger seat, the passenger seat being moveable from a stow position at an interior of the vehicle through the door opening to a deployed position at the exterior of the vehicle; wherein the passenger seat in the deployed position is disposed adjacent the PMD receiving portion in the deployed position whereby a passenger may transfer from the PMD to the passenger seat. 78. The accessible vehicle of claim 77 having a computing system configured to sequence the operation of the lifting device and the operation of the seating system to prevent the passenger seat from hitting the PMD receiving portion when deploying the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat at a pickup location. 79. The accessible vehicle of claim 78, wherein the computing system is configured to move a first of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat from the stow position toward the deployed position before moving a second of the PMD receiving portion and the lifting device from the stow position toward the deployed position. 80. The accessible vehicle of claim 79, wherein the computing system is configured to stage the first of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat adjacent the door opening prior to opening the door at the pickup location. 81. The accessible vehicle of claims 79 or 80, wherein the computing system is configured to begin moving the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat from the stow position toward the deployed position before the first of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat has reached the deployed position. 82. The accessible vehicle of claims 79 or 80, wherein the computing system is configured to begin moving the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat from the stow position toward the deployed position after the first of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat has reached the deployed position. 83. The accessible vehicle of any one of claims 79-82, wherein the computing system is configured to begin moving one of the first and the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat from the deployed position toward the stow position after detecting that the passenger has transferred from the PMD to the passenger seat. 84. The accessible vehicle of claim 83, wherein the computing system is configured to begin moving an other of the first and the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat from the deployed position toward the stow position before the one of the first and the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat reaches the stow position. 85. The accessible vehicle of claim 83, wherein the computing system is configured to begin moving an other of the first and the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat from the deployed position toward the stow position after the one of the first and the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat reaches the stow position. 86. The accessible vehicle of claim 77 having a computing system configured to sequence the operation of the lifting device and the operation of the seating system to prevent the passenger and the passenger seat from hitting the PMD and the PMD receiving portion when deploying the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat at a dropoff location. 87. The accessible vehicle of claim 86, wherein the computing system is configured to move a first of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat from the stow position toward the deployed position before moving a second of the PMD receiving portion and the lifting device from the stow position toward the deployed position. 88. The accessible vehicle of claim 87, wherein the computing system is configured to stage the first of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat adjacent the door opening prior to opening the door at the dropoff location. 89. The accessible vehicle of claims 87 or 88, wherein the computing system is configured to begin moving the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat from the stow position toward the deployed position before the first of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat has reached the deployed position. 90. The accessible vehicle of claims 87 or 88, wherein the computing system is configured to begin moving the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat from the stow position toward the deployed position after the first of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat has reached the deployed position. 91. The accessible vehicle of any one of claims 86-90, wherein the computing system is configured to begin moving the passenger seat from the deployed position toward the stow position only after detecting that the passenger has transferred from the passenger seat to the PMD. 92. The accessible vehicle of any one of claims 86-91, wherein the computing system is configured to begin moving the PMD receiving portion from the deployed position toward the stow position only after detecting that the PMD has been disengaged from the PMD receiving portion. 93. The accessible vehicle of any one of claims 86-92, wherein the computing system is configured to begin moving one of the first and the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat from the deployed position toward the stow position before moving an other of the first and the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat from the deployed position toward the stow position. 94. The accessible vehicle of claim 93, wherein the computing system is configured to begin moving the other of the first and the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat from the deployed position toward the stow position before the one of the first and the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat reaches the stow position. 95. The accessible vehicle of claim 93, wherein the computing system is configured to begin moving an other of the first and the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat from the deployed position toward the stow position after the one of the first and the second of the PMD receiving portion and the passenger seat reaches the stow position.
